title: Macaroni
description: The Macaroni class (ages 6 months to 3 years) introduces our youngest learners to a nurturing academic environment that fosters curiosity, sensory exploration and early social skills. In addition to their lessons on numbers, letters, colors and syllables, your children will enjoy a morning full of play, music and movement, obstacle courses, tumbling, classical stories and so much more in just 3 hours, 3 days a week. With day to day consistency and rhythm, your tots will benefit from a well rounded routine at ASA, created with exceptional educational activities and quality arts & crafts. Our co-op has been meticulously planned out with intention and purpose. Designed for homeschooling families, this program balances learning and living, preparing children for a lifelong love of discovery.
subject: Early Childhood Education

title: Seekers Full Day 9AM - 2PM
description: The Seekers class (grades 3–5) deepens classical education with a focus on spelling, history, and financial literacy. Montessori-inspired, hands-on activities develop critical thinking and civic responsibility, preparing students for a life of freedom and intellectual growth in a parent-driven educational model.

title: Tycoons
description:
Academy 10 Week Sessions 2025-2026
Fall 2025
Winter 2026
Spring 2026
Cost
The cost covers one full 10 week session, 3 days per week and may change. A sibling discount of 10% is applied at checkout. ‘Free After Three’ applies to the fourth child and on! 

Morning Academics Only - $900 per child
Full Day Including Afternoon Extracurriculars - $1300 per child
Mentor 
Miss Bella Proietti (Since Winter 2024)
Store Owner, ‘Oh Bella’ (Webster)
Overview
A meaningful, human connection with a teacher is an invaluable benefit to a child. We believe mentors are a necessary attribute of the traditional school model that homeschoolers can greatly benefit from. The Tycoons class (grades 1–2) dives into Literacy Essentials building a strong foundation necessary for developing reading and writing skills. Throughout a 3 hour period, 3 days a week, students receive in person instruction using structured curricula that covers all common subjects and highlights America’s founding principles. In a positive and social environment, the ASA model emphasizes self-governance, critical thinking and entrepreneurship. With day to day consistency and rhythm, your child will benefit from a well rounded routine created with exceptional educational activities and quality arts & crafts. Designed for homeschooling families, this program balances learning and living, inspiring children for a lifelong love of discovery.
Safety
Parents feel at ease knowing their children’s safety is our first priority using background checks for all volunteers and highly trained armed guards to monitor the location at all times. Complete with cameras both inside and out, ASA works with companies that come fully insured and equipped. Each family has a unique code to access the locked building and can freely check on their child as they feel necessary.



Curriculum Subjects and Resources
Literacy: Phonograms, decoding, handwriting, spelling.
Curriculum: Literacy Essentials, Primary Phonics, All About Spelling
Overview: Literacy Essentials teaches 72 phonograms and spelling rules. Primary Phonics reinforces decoding with storybooks. All About Spelling introduces spelling patterns.
Math: Addition, subtraction, number sense.
Curriculum: Dimensions Math.
Overview: Covers addition, subtraction within 100 place value, and shapes using number bonds and bar models.
Science: Plants, animals, weather.
Curriculum: My PALS Science.
Overview: Explores life sciences (plants, animals) and physical sciences (weather) through experiments and activity books.
History: Abeka’s Our America, My America and My World
Overview: Pilgrims, Founding Fathers, American growth. 
Geography: Continents, maps.
Curriculum: Maps, Globes, Graphs.
Topics: Continents, globes, map symbols.
Financial Literacy: Allowances, budgeting.
Curriculum: Tuttle Twins Economics.
Overview: Saving, spending, entrepreneurship
Art: Drawing, painting, crafts.
Curriculum: Includes How to Draw videos from Art Hub and ASA’s Portrait Series sketches
Creative Writing: Letter writing, storytelling.
Curriculum: Institute of Excellence in Writing (IEW).
Self-Governance: Decision-making, responsibility.
Curriculum: PragerU videos, moral stories
Topics: Manners, honesty, perseverance..
Physical Education: Gym games, cooperative play.
Topics: Teamwork, coordination, problem solving.

For America Founders, widespread public education was the key to equal opportunity, to individual improvement, and to the pursuit of human happiness. 
– We Still Hold These Truths
Learning Objectives
Master phonograms and spelling rules for fluent reading and writing.
Perform addition/ subtraction within 100 using Dimensions Math.
Understand plant/ animal life cycles and weather patterns.
Develop narrative writing skills using IEW.



Extracurricular Opportunities
Tycoons who register for Full Day can choose to participate in the scheduled afternoon extracurricular, Duplo Lego Robotics or Free Play. 
Sculpture
Robotics 
Architecture
Latin
Cake Decoration
Cooking and Nutrition
Junior Entrepreneur
Film
ASA Mission Alignment
The Tycoon class cultivates early civic virtue by helping foster positive interaction, demonstrating and practicing kindness and manners to prepare children for a life of freedom and intellectual growth. Your family will thrive in a community-driven setting with a focus on safety, family, comradery and academic excellence. We empower parents to choose a holistic education that nurtures their child’s potential.

  

FAQs
Q: Do I need to buy any supplies? A: We ask that each family donate a ream of printer paper at the start of each session. All other supplies are provided. There are only supply fees for afternoon extracurriculars.
Q: What do I send my Tycoon to school with? A: A backpack, snack and water bottle. Pack a lunch if they are full day.
Q: Is there homework? A: A Math Review Packet is sent home about once every week or so. Occasionally there will be a Show and Tell presentation to prepare for at home. Each session has a final project and may need to be worked on at home as well.  
Q: Is there a dress code? A: Yes. There is a basic dress code that includes khaki or navy pants, shorts or skirts and any color polo.
Q: Do I need to purchase any curriculum or books? A: No. We provide these to the students which are covered by the annual membership fee. 
Q: Are all IHIP requirements met with your program alone? A: Yes
Q: Are you accredited? A: No. We are a PMA separate from the state and approved by parents exclusively. Assessments are provided for tracking progress.
Q: Is there any religious content taught? A: We are not a faith based co-op, however, there are lessons that demonstrate the important role God plays in our American founding and how religious values and liberty are intertwined. The Pledge of allegiance is recited each morning.



Letter from the Founders
Dear Fellow Parent, 

Choosing to homeschool our own children gave us more time to spend together and an opportunity to shape their education according to our standards. We found the perfect academic components in the Classical model of education, which we believe offers the surest, time-tested and most complete schooling when paired with in-home learning. American Seekers Academy is a reflection of family-centric principles and our admiration of Hillsdale Academy and their Barney Initiative. With this example in mind, ASA was designed by homeschool parents like yourselves, people who appreciate certain aspects of traditional schooling and also deeply value the opportunity to be at home with their children. We wanted to meet more parents who are proudly raising their children according to their beliefs and whose hope is the same as ours, to protect the liberties of our children. Ultimately, we needed more than the average a co-op could provide. We found out that we weren’t the only ones. 

Thank you for the chance to raise our family alongside yours.

-The Browns (homeschooling since 2020)

title: Yankee Doodle 9AM - 12PM
description:
A growing ability to use language with power and grace is one of the surest marks of educated men and women and one of the surest safeguards against the loss of liberty.
– Quoted from Hillsdale College
Academy 10 Week Sessions 2025-2026
Fall 2025 – 9/8/25 to 11/14/25
Winter 2026 – 1/5/26 to 3/13/26
Spring 2026 –  4/6/26 to 6/12/25
Cost
The cost covers one full 10 week session, 3 days per week and may change. A sibling discount of 10% is applied at checkout. ‘Free After Three’ applies to the fourth child and on! 

Morning Academics Only - $900 per child
Full Day Including Afternoon Extracurriculars - $1300 per child
Mentor 
Mrs. Melissa Miron (Since Spring 2024)
BA in Elementary Education with a concentration in Social Studies
AAS in Equine Science
Overview
A meaningful, human connection with a teacher is an invaluable benefit to a child. We believe mentors are a necessary attribute of the traditional school model that homeschoolers can greatly benefit from. The Yankee Doodle class (ages 4–5, PreK–K) builds foundational skills through a classical education model, emphasizing literacy, counting, and responsibility. Hands-on, kinesthetic activities engage young learners, fostering a love for learning and civic virtue. The program supports homeschooling families by providing a structured yet flexible curriculum.
Safety
Parents feel at ease knowing their children’s safety is our first priority using background checks for all volunteers and highly trained armed guards to monitor the location at all times. Complete with cameras both inside and out, ASA works with companies that come fully insured and equipped. Each family has a unique code to access the locked building and can freely check on their child as they feel necessary.


IHIP Compliance
Subjects Covered: Reading, writing, spelling, language arts, mathematics, history, geography, science, health, physical education, music, visual arts.
Instructional Hours: 900 hours annually, to be scheduled by parents.
Quarterly Reports: Due [insert dates, e.g., November 15, February 15, April 30, June 30], detailing subject progress.
Materials: Literacy Essentials, Primary Phonics, Dimensions Math, Tuttle Twins, art supplies, musical instruments.
Curriculum Subjects and Resources
Numbers: Counting (1–10), number recognition.
Curriculum: Dimensions Math (Singapore Math)
Overview: Introduces number sense, counting, and basic operations through visual models
Letters: Vowel sounds, letter recognition.
Curriculum: Literacy Essentials (Logic of English), Primary Phonics
Overview: Literacy Essentials teaches 72 phonograms. Primary Phonics uses workbooks and storybooks for CVC words and blends.
Literacy: Phonics, word recognition, storytelling.
Curriculum: Literacy Essentials, Primary Phonics
Topics: Phonemic awareness, decoding CVC words, handwriting (clock, short, tall line strokes).
Math: Counting objects, basic addition.
Curriculum: Dimensions Math
Topics: Addition within 10, shapes, patterns, measurement basics.
Art: Drawing, painting, crafts.
Curriculum: ASA exclusive content
Topics: Fine motor skills, color theory, seasonal themes.
History: Revolutionary figures, American symbols.
Curriculum: Our America (Abeka)
Overview: Covers American symbols (e.g., flag), founding figures (e.g., Washington, Hamilton), and key events with a patriotic focus.
Geography: Simple maps, neighborhood concepts.
Curriculum: Maps, Globes, Graphs (Steck-Vaughn).
Overview: Introduces globes, maps, and directional concepts through hands-on activities.
Financial Literacy: Coin values, basic money concepts.
Curriculum: Tuttle Twins Economics.
Overview: Teaches coin recognition and basic economic principles (e.g., trade, value).
Science: Sensory experiments, spring-themed activities.
Curriculum: Hands-on experiments (e.g., cloud in a jar, ice fishing).
Topics: Weather, sensory properties, cause-and-effect.
Self-Governance: Responsibility, manners.
Curriculum: Manners Poem, behavior books (e.g., No, David!)
Topics: Sharing, respect, following rules.
Health: Hygiene, safety practices.
Curriculum: Mentor-led discussions, stories
Topics: Handwashing, safe play.
Physical Education: Cooperative games, gym activities.
Curriculum: Games like scooter tag, parachute popcorn.
Topics: Coordination, teamwork, gross motor skills.
Music: Songs, rhythm activities.
Curriculum: Songs like Days of the Week, DJ Raphi’s Freeze Dance, etc.


For America Founders, widespread public education was the key to equal opportunity, to individual improvement, and to the pursuit of human happiness. 
– We Still Hold These Truths
Learning Objectives
Master short vowel sounds and decode CVC words using Literacy Essentials.
Count to 10 and perform basic addition with Dimensions Math.
Understand American symbols and figures (e.g., flag, Washington).
Develop responsibility through manners and cooperative activities.
Enhance fine motor skills through art and handwriting.
Teaching Methods
Interactive Kinesthetic: Jumping for vowel sounds, syllable drumming.
Project-Based: Creating maps, snowman art.
Discussion-Based: Circle time discussions on manners, history.
Hands-On: Experiments like cloud in a jar, sensory bins.
Sample Activities
Counting Nature Walk: Collect 1–5 leaves/stones, counting aloud (Week 2).
Literacy Station: Trace letter “a” with clock strokes, read Mac and Tab (Week 1).
Art Project: Create puffy snow paint snowmen (Week 2).
History Lesson: Cut and glue $10 bill with Hamilton’s face (Week 2).
Science Experiment: Cloud in a jar with hot water and ice (Week 2).
Assessments
Weekly phonogram and number recognition checks.
Project completion (e.g., maps, art).
Progress reports (Week 8) on social and academic growth.
Portfolio of handwriting, art, and experiment observations.
Extracurricular Opportunities
Yankee Doodles who are registered for Full Day will enjoy a lunch period followed by recess and an hour long of their choice of free play or a Lego Duplo Robotics!
ASA Mission Alignment
Yankee Doodle fosters a limitless mentality through engaging, structured activities, ensuring parents’ rights to choose a holistic education that prepares children for intellectual and civic growth.


FAQs
Q: Do I need to buy any supplies? A: We ask that each family donate a ream of printer paper at the start of each session. All other supplies are provided. There are only supply fees for afternoon extracurriculars.
Q: What do I send my Yankee doodle to school with? A: A backpack, snack and water bottle. Pack a lunch if they are full day.
Q: Is there homework? A: A Math Review Packet is sent home about once every week or so. Occasionally there will be a Show and Tell presentation to prepare for at home. Each session has a final project and may need to be worked on at home as well.  
Q: Is there a dress code? A: Yes. There is a basic dress code that includes khaki or navy pants, shorts or skirts and any color polo.
Q: Do I need to purchase any curriculum or books? A: No. We provide these to the students which are covered by the annual membership fee. 
Q: Are all IHIP requirements met with your program alone? A: Yes
Q: Are you accredited? A: No. We are a PMA separate from the state and approved by parents exclusively. Assessments are provided for tracking progress.
Q: Is there any religious content taught? A: We are not a faith based co-op, however, there are lessons that demonstrate the important role God plays in our American founding and how religious values and liberty are intertwined. The Pledge of allegiance is recited each morning.



Letter from the Founders
Dear Fellow Parent, 

Choosing to homeschool our own children gave us more time to spend together and an opportunity to shape their education according to our standards. We found the perfect academic components in the Classical model of education, which we believe offers the surest, time-tested and most complete schooling when paired with in-home learning. American Seekers Academy is a reflection of family-centric principles and our admiration of Hillsdale Academy and their Barney Initiative. With this example in mind, ASA was designed by homeschool parents like yourselves, people who appreciate certain aspects of traditional schooling and also deeply value the opportunity to be at home with their children. We wanted to meet more parents who are proudly raising their children according to their beliefs and whose hope is the same as ours, to protect the liberties of our children. Ultimately, we needed more than the average a co-op could provide. We found out that we weren’t the only ones. 

Thank you for the chance to raise our family alongside yours.

-The Browns (homeschooling since 2020)

title: Macaroni
description:
A growing ability to use language with power and grace is one of the surest marks of educated men and women and one of the surest safeguards against the loss of liberty.
– Quoted from Hillsdale College

Academy 10 Week Sessions 2025-2026
Fall 2025 – 9/8/25 to 11/14/25
Winter 2026 – 1/5/26 to 3/13/26
Spring 2026 –  4/6/26 to 6/12/25
Cost
The cost covers one full 10 week session, 3 days per week and may change. A sibling discount of 10% is applied at checkout. ‘Free After Three’ applies to the fourth child and on! 

Morning Academics Only - $900 per child
Full Day Including Afternoon Extracurriculars - $1300 per child
Mentor 
Mrs. Leigh Ann Borelli (Since Fall 2024)
Little People Whisperer
Overview
A meaningful, human connection with a teacher is an invaluable benefit to a child. We believe mentors are a necessary attribute of the traditional school model that homeschoolers can greatly benefit from. The Macaroni class (ages 6 months to 3 years) introduces our youngest learners to a nurturing academic environment that fosters curiosity, sensory exploration and early social skills. In addition to their lessons on numbers, letters, colors and syllables, your children will enjoy a morning full of play, music and movement, obstacle courses, tumbling, classical stories and so much more in just 3 hours, 3 days a week. With day to day consistency and rhythm, your tots will benefit from a well rounded routine at ASA, created with exceptional educational activities and quality arts & crafts. Our co-op has been meticulously planned out with intention and purpose. Designed for homeschooling families, this program balances learning and living, preparing children for a lifelong love of discovery.
Safety
Parents feel at ease knowing their children’s safety is our first priority using background checks for all volunteers and highly trained armed guards to monitor the location at all times. Complete with cameras both inside and out, ASA works with companies that come fully insured and equipped. Each family has a unique code to access the locked building and can freely check on their child as they feel necessary.



Curriculum Subjects and Resources
Numbers: Counting, number recognition using number cards and puzzles, songs and games.
Letters: Alphabet introduction, letter sounds using ABC cards, puzzles, songs and games.
Colors: Color identification, sorting with sensory bins and flashcards, poems and more.
Literacy: Story time, rhyming, basic phonics through picture books, songs and syllables games.
Math: Shapes, patterns, basic counting using a myriad of materials and manipulatives.
Art: Creative expression through drawing, painting, and crafts.
Science & Technology: Sensory-based exploration and hands-on science experiments.
Music & Movement: Songs, parachute play, rhythm, tempo and coordination.
History: Community helpers, family roles through stories and role-play, and American symbols.
Geography: Classroom and community maps, spatial awareness and landmarks.
Financial Literacy: Coin identification and basic exchange concepts through dramatic play.
Self-Governance: Behavior books, sharing and listening exercises, practice with basic manners.
Physical Education: Gross motor activities and songs, parachute play, obstacles, gym games and more.
Learning Objectives
Develop fine and gross motor skills through sensory and movement activities.
Recognize numbers 1–20, basic letters, colors, animals and shapes.
Build social skills like sharing and listening during circle time.
Foster curiosity and creativity through hands-on science and crafts.
Understand basic community roles and civic responsibilities.
Methods of Instructing
Interactive: Songs, rhymes, and group activities like parachute play.
Project-Based: Creating holiday gifts, art projects and simple experiments.
Discussion-Based: Circle time stories and manners discussions.
Sample Activities
Counting Game: Count how many apples you can find hidden around the room.
Story Time: Read The Opposite Zoo, discuss opposites and lift flaps.
Color Sorting: Match circle stickers to matching color dot.
Art Project: Create polar bear handprints with white paint on cardstock.
Music & Movement: Dance with scarves to Laurie Berkner songs.
ASA Mission Alignment
The Macaroni class cultivates early civic virtue by helping foster positive interaction, demonstrating and practicing kindness and manners to prepare children for a life of freedom and intellectual growth. Your family will thrive in a community-driven setting with a focus on safety, family, comradery and academic excellence. We empower parents to choose a holistic education that nurtures their child’s potential.


FAQs
Q: Do I need to buy any supplies? A: We ask that each family donate a ream of printer paper at the start of each session. All other supplies are provided. There are only supply fees for afternoon extracurriculars.
Q: What do I send my Macaroni to school with? A: Snack, lunch and water bottle, preferred diapers and wipes as well as a change of clothes.
Q: Is there a dress code? A: Yes. There is a basic dress code that includes khaki or navy pants, shorts or skirts and any color polo.
Q: Are all IHIP requirements met with your program alone? A: Yes but the IHIP starts at 6 years and up.

Letter from the Founders
Dear Fellow Parent, 

Choosing to homeschool our own children gave us more time to spend together and an opportunity to shape their education according to our standards. We found the perfect academic components in the Classical model of education, which we believe offers the surest, time-tested and most complete schooling when paired with in-home learning. American Seekers Academy is a reflection of family-centric principles and our admiration of Hillsdale Academy and their Barney Initiative. With this example in mind, ASA was designed by homeschool parents like yourselves, people who appreciate certain aspects of traditional schooling and also deeply value the opportunity to be at home with their children. We wanted to meet more parents who are proudly raising their children according to their beliefs and whose hope is the same as ours, to protect the liberties of our children. Ultimately, we needed more than the average a co-op could provide. We found out that we weren’t the only ones. 

Thank you for the chance to raise our family alongside yours.

-The Browns (homeschooling since 2020)
